title: Dropdown Label Colors
excerpt:
Currently using Monday.com, to sort through 3rd party resellers. We have partners who use our products and those who don’t. 
Right now I have a dropdown column with labels that state what they use. But it would be great …

posts.author: rdeelen (Rohan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently using Monday.com, to sort through 3rd party resellers. We have partners who use our products and those who don’t.

Right now I have a dropdown column with labels that state what they use. But it would be great if I could give each label a different colour, making it easier to identify what products they are using as it could be more than one.

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml:
You could do this with the Status or the Tags column. However they both have downsides.

  • Status column only allows you to use a color once. So if you made product 1 red, you can’t use red for product 2 that may be in the same category.

  • Tags column is auto-color coordinated. I haven’t figured out how to change the color of the tags. Regardless clicking on a tag will take you to a new window. The reason it does this is because tags can span across multiple boards.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jaewoo:

how to change the color of the tags.

See “How to edit a tag”

Support

The Tags Column

3 min read Feature   The Tags column allows you to list a number of keywords that will appear next to a hashtag symbol (#) in the Tags Column. This way, whenever you create a Tags Column for a ...

Although the color options are limited, changing tag colors can be done via Search Everything > Tags, click the Pencil icon (which is only visible when you mouseover a tag):

posts.author: rdeelen (Rohan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi John, thanks for the comment, I will start to use this for now.

posts.author: Toploader (Jon Merchant)
posts.cookedHtml: Bumping an old thread to say I’d also really like to be able to do this (I use click up for another project and it allows you to color labels). I’d rather not use tags for this as a work around.

posts.author: jdorsey (Jon Dorsey)
posts.cookedHtml: Unable to change tag colors as it appears colors are hidden by text box. Same issue in app and different browsers.
